title: teucrium multiflorum var. angustifolium
intro: Teucrium multiflorum var. angustifolium, also known as the narrow-leaved Jerusalem tea or stinking foxglove, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include being antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Teucrium multiflorum var. angustifolium include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized for various health conditions.
